title: Initial Database Snapshot Flow
class_hierarchy: BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource extends RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource
interaction_flow: BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource → RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource: super.execute() → doExecute(context, previousOffset, snapshotContext, snapshottingTask)
RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource → BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource: prepare(partition, onDemand) → new BinlogSnapshotContext(partition, '')
RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource → RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource: determineCapturedTables() applies table/database filters → ctx.capturedTables, ctx.capturedSchemaTables
RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource → RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource: createConnectionPool(ctx) — builds JdbcConnection pool
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource → BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource: globalLock() [if global lock requested]
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource → BinlogConnectorConnection: executeWithoutCommitting('FLUSH TABLES WITH READ LOCK')
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource → BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource: startLockHeartbeat() — schedules SELECT 1 every 30s
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource → BinlogConnectorConnection: executeWithoutCommitting('START TRANSACTION WITH CONSISTENT SNAPSHOT') [if useConsistentSnapshotTransaction]
--- OR: tableLock() if global lock unavailable ---
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource → BinlogConnectorConnection: executeWithoutCommitting('FLUSH TABLES <list> WITH READ LOCK')
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource → BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource: tryStartingSnapshot(ctx) → offset marks snapshot started
RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource → BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource: readTableStructure(context, ctx, previousOffset, snapshottingTask)
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource → BinlogConnectorConnection: readCharsetSystemVariables() + setStatementFor() → addSchemaEvent for charset
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource [for each table]: addSchemaEvent(ctx, catalog, 'DROP TABLE IF EXISTS ...')
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource [for each table]: createSchemaEventsForTables() → connection.query('SHOW CREATE TABLE ...')
→ addSchemaEvent() → BinlogDatabaseSchema: parseSnapshotDdl(partition, ddl, database, offset, time)
--- Two-phase locking path: tableLock() + determineSnapshotOffset() called here for non-global-lock case ---
-- STEP 7: Persist schema history (schema change events) --
→ EventDispatcher: dispatchSchemaChangeEvent(partition, offset, tableId, receiver -> receiver.schemaChangeEvent(event))
RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource → RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource: createDataEvents(context, ctx, connectionPool, overrides)
→ [for each row] EventDispatcher: dispatchSnapshotEvent(partition, tableId, changeRecordEmitter, snapshotReceiver)
→ [per chunk] EventDispatcher: dispatchSnapshotEvent() with FIRST/LAST markers
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource [if globally locked]: globalUnlock() → stopLockHeartbeat() + executeWithoutCommitting('UNLOCK TABLES')
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource [if tables locked]: tableUnlock() → stopLockHeartbeat() + executeWithoutCommitting('UNLOCK TABLES')
key_relationships: BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource depends_on BinlogConnectorConnection (JDBC ops, lock execution)
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource depends_on BinlogDatabaseSchema (DDL parsing, schema event storage)
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource depends_on EventDispatcher (schema + data event emission)
BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource depends_on SnapshotterService (lock strategy, snapshot query, snapshot decision)
RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource orchestrates BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource via Template Method pattern
BinlogDatabaseSchema uses DdlParser to parse DDL into SchemaChangeEvents
BinlogOffsetContext tracks: restartBinlogFilename, restartBinlogPosition, restartGtidSet, inTransaction
important_notes: Template Method pattern: RelationalSnapshotChangeEventSource.doExecute() calls abstract hooks implemented by BinlogSnapshotChangeEventSource
Lock strategy is pluggable via SnapshotterService.getSnapshotLock(): global lock (FLUSH TABLES WITH READ LOCK) preferred, falls back to per-table FLUSH TABLES ... WITH READ LOCK
Lock heartbeat: SELECT 1 every 30s keeps connection alive during long snapshots (binlogConnectionMutex guards concurrent access)
Two-phase schema snapshot: if global lock unavailable, schema captured in two rounds — tables locked individually, second phase after data unlock
Offset determination captures current binlog filename+position (and GTID set if enabled) WHILE lock is held to ensure consistency
Data snapshot supports two modes: legacy (one thread per table) and chunked (parallel chunks with boundary calculation)
delayedSchemaSnapshotTables: tables whose schema is captured after data lock release to minimize lock hold time
BinlogSnapshotContext inner class: extends RelationalSnapshotContext with empty catalogName
BinlogOffsetContext key fields: restartBinlogFilename, restartBinlogPosition, restartGtidSet, currentGtidSet, restartEventsToSkip, restartRowsToSkip
BinlogDatabaseSchema.parseSnapshotDdl() → parseDdl(snapshot=true): parses DDL via ddlParser.parse(), emits typed SchemaChangeEvents (CREATE/ALTER/DROP/TRUNCATE/DATABASE)
